title: Commentary on Part VI (A New Energy Security Strategy)
summary: For more than a century, energy and its procurement have been central to the U.S. position as a world power.
body: Overview Part VI (A New Energy Security Strategy) of Energy & Security: Toward a New Foreign Policy Strategy consists of the following chapters: 17— Energy, Homeland, and National Security by Leon Fuerth 18—Technology Development and Energy Security by Melanie A. Kenderdine and Ernest J. Moniz 19—Governance, Transparency, and Sustainable Development by Charles McPherson 20—The Challenge of Climate Protection: Balancing Energy and Environment by Kevin A. Baumert 21—Building Strategic Reserves by David L. Goldwyn and Michelle Billig 22—Can a "Global" Natural Gas Market Be Achieved? by Donald A. Juckett and Michelle Michot Foss The latest from Belfer See more Recommended citation Holdren, John. “Commentary on Part VI (A New Energy Security Strategy).” 2005 Copy to Clipboard Commentary on Part VI (A New Energy Security Strategy) View Citation Print Energy Energy Innovation Policy Energy Technology Innovation Policy Governance International Relations International Security & Defense Middle East & North Africa Middle East Policy National security economics Nuclear Issues Nuclear Waste Nuclear power Oil Science, Technology, and Public Policy Security Strategy U.S. Foreign Policy Recommended citation Holdren, John. “Commentary on Part VI (A New Energy Security Strategy).” 2005 Copy to Clipboard Author John P.
